threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's 'going to the tube' instead of 'the Overground.' It’s a subtle but telling slip around here.
Last night on the 63, a guy was trying to sell 'vintage' records. He kept calling them ‘bangers’ but they were all scratched-up Cher CDs.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the kebab shop on Karl-Marx-Straße remembered my order and asked about my cat – that’s when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find kids playing in the street while parents sip beer on the sidewalk, no fences, just pure, chaotic freedom.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell someone’s from here when they warn you about the BART delays before you even mention your commute. It’s like a badge of survival.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy board the 31 with a full-sized surfboard today. The driver just shook his head and let him on. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think we all dine at fancy restaurants, but the real gems are the corner bars where locals grab a quick bocadillo between 3-5 PM.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring at the metro map like it’s hieroglyphics, while the locals glide past, fully confident in their route.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You ask for directions to the bus stop and a local sends you to the nearest pub instead. Classic Dalston hospitality.
Caught a late night bus from Brixton, and the driver was belting out Elton John. Never seen so many people singing along at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I saw an old man in a bathrobe arguing with a pigeon on Karl-Marx-Strasse. That’s when I knew this place was special.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see spontaneous parties in abandoned buildings, complete with DJs and makeshift bars. It’s a unique slice of urban life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to a BART delay—rolling eyes and a deep sigh, not panic like the tourists. It’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling flaming batons on the 14! Only in SF do you see street performances spill into public transit. Wild.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think ‘Gràcia’ is just another Gaudí spot, but we actually love the local bars and the underground music scene. They miss the heart of it.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order a 'café con leche'—it’s always a bit too loud and too much enthusiasm for the locals' morning routine.
